steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $12x^3y + 30xy^2$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute $6xy$ across the parentheses
steps.content: Multiply each term inside by $6xy$: $$6xy \cdot 2x^2 = 12x^3 y \qquad \text{and} \qquad 6xy \cdot 5y = 30xy^2.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$x^3y + 11xy^2$’: $x^3y + 11xy^2$ — missing the coefficient $12$ and incorrectly adds $12+30$ into $11xy^2$. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — ‘$x^2y + 5y$’: $x^2y + 5y$ — ignores the $6xy$ factor entirely. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — ‘$12x^2y + 30xy$’: $12x^2y + 30xy$ — wrong power of $x$ in the first term and wrong power of $y$ in the second. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — ‘$12x^3y + 30xy^2$’: $12x^3y + 30xy^2$ — matches the distribution exactly. Correct.
That confirms D is correct.
